downloads_disabled_banner: Charging and subscription invoice downloads are both disabled — syncs only verify the Tesla connection; no invoices will be downloaded. Enable at least one invoice type in the app configuration to resume downloads.
chart_empty: No data for the current filters.
no_invoices: No invoices found. Run "Sync all history" on the Maintenance tab to fetch older invoices.
no_match: No invoices match the current filters.
no_token: No Tesla account connected yet. Use "Connect Tesla account" on the Maintenance tab (or the banner above) to get started.
load_failed: Failed to load data. See browser console for details.
files_select: Select a file to preview its contents.
files_empty: No files have been stored yet.
maint_sync_text: New invoices for the current and previous month are fetched automatically. A full sync downloads the entire charging and subscription history and can take a while. If the automatic email export is enabled, you will be asked whether the invoices found by this sync should also be emailed (default: no).
maint_backlog_text: Invoices synced while email export was disabled (or during a sync with email sending skipped) are marked as skipped and never emailed automatically. Send them here as a combined export — batched into a few emails instead of one mail per invoice.
maint_rescan_text: Re-extracts cost and currency from the stored subscription PDFs with the current parser. Useful after an app update when older metadata contains wrong values. Charging figures come from the Tesla API and are refreshed by a sync instead.
sync_started: Sync started – this can take a while.
rescan_conflict: A sync is running — try again when it has finished.
dlg_backlog_msg: Send all skipped invoices (combined) by email to:
invalid_email: Please enter a valid email address.
dlg_sync_msg: Download the complete invoice history? This can take a while.
dlg_sync_check: Also email each new invoice found by this sync (unchecked: mark them as skipped, sendable later via "Email backlog")
dlg_delete_pdf:
Delete "{file}"?

Its metadata entry is deleted too, and the PDF will be downloaded again on the next sync as long as its invoice exists at Tesla.
dlg_delete_json:
Delete "{file}"?

This is a metadata file: without it the invoice looks brand new again — if automatic email export is enabled, it may be emailed a second time.
setup_banner_text: Welcome! Connect your Tesla account to start downloading your invoices — no configuration needed.
account_text: Sign in with your Tesla account to let the app download your invoices. The login happens on Tesla's own website; only the resulting token is stored (with strict file permissions) and used to fetch invoices. Your password is never seen by this app.
auth_step_open: Open the Tesla login in a new tab and sign in with your Tesla account. A security check may appear — that is Tesla's, not this app's.
auth_step_error_page: After signing in, the browser shows an error or an empty page ("Page Not Found"). That is expected — the login worked; the page only fails because it tries to open the Tesla mobile app.
auth_step_copy: Copy the full address of that page from the address bar — it starts with "tesla://auth/callback" — and paste it below:
auth_hint_title: Can't find the address? (common on desktop browsers)
auth_hint_text: Some desktop browsers never show the tesla:// address in the address bar. Right-click the error page and choose "Inspect", open the "Console" tab, and look for a link starting with "tesla://auth/callback?code=" (it also appears on the "Network" tab). Copy that whole link and paste it below. If that is too fiddly, switch to "Paste refresh token" above.
auth_token_text: If the browser sign-in does not work, you can create a refresh token with a trusted third-party tool — e.g. tesla_auth (Windows/macOS/Linux) or the "Auth app for Tesla" (iOS) — and paste it here. The token is verified with Tesla before it is stored.
auth_token_placeholder: Paste your Tesla refresh token (starts with "eyJ…")
auth_no_code: That address does not contain a login code. After signing in, copy the full address starting with "tesla://auth/callback" from the address bar.
